slide-1.text:
in these situations, we should instruct them that I can't edit existing songs, only help them make new changes with their changes, which unfortunately aren't guaranteed to sound just like the previous, but hopefully they will still like, maybe even more! but we should admit its a bummer. 

since song generations come in 4's, if they've made more than 4 songs for a single recipient, then we know they've used the edit feature. if they only have 4, then we may need to teach them how to use it. this will let them change the lyrics or the style. they can edit the lyrics manually via the button at the bottom of the review songs page, or they can use the AI edit feature where they can just describe their changes.

slide-2.text:
We should relate to them, if they just said a rly brief thank you! Keep our response simple, my pleasure, hope they love their song! Kinda one off quick thing.

If they expressed deeper gratitude, we should respond a little more and tell them its our pleasure and that it means a lot. empathy for their situation if they are expressing something hard, and that its honor to be able to help in a small way. stay humble. but not say more than they did. keep it relatively short, like heartfelt but in a bit of a rush.

slide-3.text: we should look up their email and see if they have songs. if they are referring to a specific one that is unlocked, we should send a link to that specific song.
